items.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba and Baidu
items.content: The deal, which was rumored to be in the works last year, marks an important step for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.

items.title: India bets billions on breaking China’s grip on smartphone manufacturing
items.content: New Delhi announced a $6.5 billion smartphone manufacturing program and a $13.3 billion semiconductor push to deepen India's electronics supply chain.

items.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
items.content: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

items.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II
items.content: The vulnerability in the decades-old game could have allowed hackers to take over victims’ computers with a malicious game invite.

items.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.content: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

items.title: Roger Summit, Who Invented an Early Online Search Service, Dies at 95
items.content: A research scientist at Lockheed, he came up with the idea for a computer system that would search scientific and technical literature almost instantaneously.

items.title: States Rush to Fill Graduate Loan Gap Opened by G.O.P. Budget Bill
items.content: Minnesota and Connecticut are two of the states expanding their loan options. “We see it as a substitute program,” one official said.

items.title: Stocks Sink on Anxiety About Tech and A.I. Spending
items.content: A sell-off in Asia led by chipmakers spilled into Europe and U.S. markets on Friday.
